purpose: Map common MoonCat technical tasks to the minimum repo files an agent should load.

notes: Use primaryFiles first and load optionalFiles only when the task needs explanation, provenance, or cross-checking.
This index routes to existing files and does not duplicate endpoint, contract, source, identifier, trait, or membership data.
If a referenced data file marks a category incomplete, preserve that incomplete status.
Use data/task-recipes.json when the task needs a workflow sequence, guardrails, or stop conditions across multiple files.

tasks.description: Find workflow sequences, guardrails, and stop conditions for common MoonCat KB tasks.
notes: Use recipes for sequencing existing files, not as a replacement for the underlying data files.
Recipes do not add endpoint, contract, trait, color, identifier, source, or per-cat facts.

tasks.description: Find current KB usability gaps, resolved gaps, routing coverage, and recommended next passes.
notes: Use this route for planning, audits, gap review, and next-pass prioritization.
Do not treat gap notes as MoonCat facts; use the referenced data files and sources for technical claims.

tasks.description: Choose the correct MoonCat identifier form and understand conversion limits.
notes: Do not treat rescue indexes, token IDs, OpenSea IDs, and bytes5 catIds as interchangeable.
Use docs only when the JSON summary is not enough to explain a conversion or limitation.

tasks.description: Find API paths, methods, accepted identifier forms, params, and response kinds.
notes: Use the endpoint manifest as a routing aid, not a substitute for full OpenAPI schemas.
Check identifier conventions before constructing MoonCat endpoint parameters.

tasks.description: Plan trait lookups and understand current trait data coverage.
notes: Machine-readable per-cat traits are limited to the generated 64-row representative sample in data/.
Do not treat the sample as full-population coverage or import the full upstream trait table without a focused generated-data pass.

tasks.description: Regenerate, validate, or interpret the bounded per-cat visual-trait prototype.
notes: The artifact has 64 deterministic representative rows and is not complete population coverage.
Use the generator --check mode and dedicated validator; do not hand-edit rows.
Preserve direct, normalized, derived, and unresolved provenance categories and all mismatch originals.

tasks.description: Find image-rendering API endpoints and image-related identifier requirements.
notes: Endpoint image response types and query parameters live in data/api-endpoints.json.
Parser, library, or on-chain SVG behavior should be checked through optional context before making implementation claims.

tasks.description: Find known contract entries, contract-derived constants, and source provenance.
notes: Do not invent or fill missing contract addresses.
Use sources.json before relying on non-obvious contract claims.

tasks.description: Find curated sourceRefs, trust notes, and reference handling policy.
notes: Add or update source entries only in a source-focused pass.
If sources conflict, document the conflict instead of choosing silently.

tasks.description: Review trait-data strategy, observed upstream shape, and promotion limits.
notes: This task intentionally loads the Markdown strategy because curated machine-readable trait data is incomplete.
Avoid promoting full upstream mappings without a separate documented import and validation pass.

tasks.description: Find community-curated character-cat definitions, exact memberships, and related hue notes.
notes: Character-cat data is community-curated; preserve trust and coverage notes from the referenced files.
Use the index file for exact membership numbers rather than copying lists into prompts.

tasks.description: Find rescue/history bucket membership arrays and related identifier conventions.
notes: Use the bucket file for existing membership arrays.
Confirm index kind before mixing bucket memberships with API parameters or token-facing identifiers.

tasks.description: Review original browser-side normal MoonCat rescue/mining seed search, the wallet-free snippet, and the embeddable widget example.
notes: Use this route for searchSeed, seed candidate generation, keccak valid-hash checks, catId derivation, and rescueCat transaction-boundary context.
The snippet and widget are wallet-free and network-free; do not add private-key, provider, account, gas, or transaction-submission code.
The widget renders found catIds with mooncatparser.js, but do not infer traits, ownership, rescue-order membership, current availability, or chain state from rescue mining alone.

tasks.description: Find exact MoonCatAccessories definition, management, eligibility, assignment, ownership, wear-state, verification, and identifier semantics.
notes: Keep definition management by wallet separate from accessory ownership by rescue order and Acclimated ERC-721 action authorization.
Do not treat accessoryId, ownedAccessoryIndex, paletteIndex, zIndex, rescueOrder, bytes5 catId, or wrapper token IDs as interchangeable.
The review records source capabilities only; do not infer current definitions, ownership, supply, approval, eligibility, or administration.

tasks.description: Find top-level MoonCat categories, special-category scaffolds, and color/hue context.
notes: Respect incomplete category markers.
Use character-cat and hue files only when the classification task needs those narrower datasets.

tasks.description: Find hue/coating scaffolds, character hue notes, and color-comparison method context.
notes: Use data/color-source-inventory.json before changing canonical color/hue data or claiming rendering behavior.
Do not infer exact trait mappings from hue notes unless the referenced file explicitly provides them.
Use docs/color-math.md only when method explanation is needed.

tasks.description: Find curated CID placeholders, project links, and preserved navigation links.
notes: Do not promote preserved navigation links into canonical sources without a source-focused review.
Use data/project-links.json trustLevel, ownershipStatus, and trustNotes for classified links; use data/link-index.json for broader preserved research links.
Leave unknown CIDs or URLs incomplete rather than guessing.
